title: 15.-Contract: BayGreen, Inc. for Mobile Pumpout and Related Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager or designee to execute a contract and
any amendments with BayGreen, Inc. for mobile vessel pumpout and related services at
the Berkeley Marina in an amount not to exceed $195,898 for a period of three years,
with an option to renew for an additional two years not to exceed $150,725, for a total
contract amount not to exceed $346,623.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funds for this contract are available in the FY25 Marina Fund (Fund 608). Budget
allocations for future fiscal years are subject to adoption of the budget by the City
Council.
sections.current_situation:
The current contract for mobile vessel pumpout services with BayGreen, Inc. terminates
on July 1, 2025. In February of 2025, to prepare for a new contract for these services,
the City conducted a competitive Request for Proposal (RFP) process to solicit boat
pumpout services at the Berkeley Marina (Specification No. 25-11709-C). On April 1,
2025, the City received one bid, from Bay Green, Inc. Staff have determined that
BayGreen has provided reliable boat pumpout services at the Berkeley Marina in the
past, and the pricing schedule and contract amount is reasonable. Therefore, staff
recommends that a new contract be authorized.
sections.background:
For several decades, the Berkeley Marina has provided free monthly vessel pumpout
services for existing berthers as part of the monthly berther fee. The City has contracted
with BayGreen for this service since 2011.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
Current regulations prohibit the discharge of sewage from boats in the San Francisco
Bay for environmental and public health reasons. The provision of this amenity to

sections.rationale:
The City conducted a competitive Request for Proposal (RFP) process to obtain boat
pumpout services at the Berkeley Marina. BayGreen meets the criteria contained in the
RFP at a reasonable cost.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation & Waterfront Department, 981-6700
Alexandra Endress, Waterfront Manager, 981-6737
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT: MOBILE PUMPOUT AND RELATED SERVICES WITH BAYGREEN,
INC.
WHEREAS, the Parks, Recreation and Waterfront Department contracts out for mobile
vessel pumpout services for Berkeley Marina berthers as an amenity and to discourage
the illegal dumping of sewage and gray water into City waters and San Francisco Bay;
and
WHEREAS, the current contract with BayGreen, Inc. for the provision of mobile vessel
pumpout and related services will terminate as of July 1, 2025; and
WHEREAS, in February of 2025, to prepare for a new contract for mobile pumpout and
related services, the City conducted a competitive bidding process to mobile pumpout
and related services (Specification No. 25-11709-C). On April 1, 2025, the City received
one bid. Staff have determined that Bay Green has provided reliable boat pumpout
services at the Berkeley Marina in the past, and the pricing schedule and contract amount
for the provision of these services is reasonable; and
WHEREAS, funding for this contract is available in the Marina Fund (Fund 608) and
budget allocations for future fiscal years are subject to adoption of the budget by the City
Council.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager or designee is hereby authorized to execute a contract and any
amendments with BayGreen, Inc. in an amount not to exceed $195,898 for a period of
three years, with an option to renew for an additional two years not to exceed $150,725,
for a total contract amount not to exceed $346,623 contingent upon annual budget
appropriation. A record signature copy of said contract will be on file in the Office of the
City Clerk.
